data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: After years of mobile service, Cheez'd and Confuzed is opening a stationary location. Owner Daniel Ray is also adding a new brand, Empire Steak South, to their mobile food lineup.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.status: adding a new brand to their mobile food lineup
data.short_description: Empire Steak South is a new mobile brand being added by the owner of Cheez'd and Confuzed. Both concepts will be stationed at the Ball Ground address.

data.location: 2110 Powers Ferry Rd Suite 120, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.short_description: Big Fat Vegan is a vegan concept from mother-daughter team Tanetta Ellzey and Iyonna Poags. This marks the brand's first permanent location after operating as a food truck and pop-up for two to three years.

data.status: targeting a late May to early June opening
data.short_description: Pataaka is a new modern Indian restaurant from Anish Nair, designed as an upscale dining destination. The space will feature warm lighting, contemporary design, approximately 113 seats, a mezzanine for private dining, and a 14-seat bar.

data.store_name: SKOL Brewing Company (Valhalla Gaming Lounge expansion)
data.short_description: SKOL Brewing Company is expanding its existing 5,000 sq ft brewpub with a 30,000 sq ft addition called Valhalla Gaming Lounge. The expansion will feature 40+ gaming options, live music, large-format TVs, and a new commercial kitchen.

data.event_date: Late May to early June (grand opening)
data.status: aiming for a soft opening in the coming weeks and a grand opening toward the end of May or early June
data.short_description: The Energy Station is a nutrition shop offering healthy shakes, energy refreshers, fruit bowls, waffles, and donuts. This will be the third location for the business group, owned by Samuel Bustamante.
Fast-casual deli from Sarah and Barrett Dabbs taking over a former Smoothie King with a drive-thru. Menu centers on hot and cold sandwiches, soups, salads, and high-protein options.

data.location: 42 Union St S Suite B, Concord, NC 28025
data.status: Targeting late June to early July opening
data.short_description: Full-service Caribbean fusion restaurant with lounge atmosphere, indoor seating, and patio from Jeanneth Vasquez and Demonde Richardson. Menu draws from Jamaican, Puerto Rican, and Honduran influences with a full bar.

data.location: 360 Exchange St NW Suite 107, Concord, NC 28027
data.status: Targeting opening in about two months
data.short_description: Second location of a full-service breakfast and brunch concept from owner Jose Fuentes, featuring a light remodel. Menu includes waffles, pancakes, chicken and waffles, sandwiches, and burgers; planned daily 7 a.m.–3 p.m.
Latin restaurant planned for Concord from owner Isidra Barron Meza. Hours are to be announced.

data.status: opening in the former location of Smoothie King
data.short_description: Fast-casual deli from Sarah and Barrett Dabbs, who also own Johnny Roger's BBQ & Burgers nearby. Taking over a former Smoothie King space with a drive-thru.

data.status: targeting an opening in the coming weeks
data.short_description: New restaurant and live music venue from musicians Buddy Black and John Forbes focused on New York-style pizza and a full bar. The space is expected to accommodate around 300 guests indoors with additional outdoor seating.

data.status: about 85% ready and working through final inspections before opening
data.short_description: The Jugo Bar is opening its first street-level retail location after years of production and wholesale operations at the same address. The roughly 3,000 sq ft space will offer fresh juices, smoothies, smoothie bowls, algae-based offerings, and rooibos teas.

Crisp Kitchen & Cocktails relocated from Latta Arcade to a new space in Brevard Courtyard approximately 100 feet away, with lunch service beginning March 5. Dinner and cocktail service is expected to launch in early April.

data.location: 7808 Rea Rd Suite E, Charlotte, NC 28277
data.status: targeting an opening in approximately six to seven weeks
data.short_description: Palm Berries, an açaí bowl and smoothie concept founded by Chandler Gottlieb, Erum Hussain, Ahsan Mehmood, and Noor Mehmood, is preparing to open a new Ballantyne-area location. Construction is in progress.

Dedicated article on The Tickled Pickle Deli from owners Sarah and Barrett Dabbs, taking over a former Smoothie King with a drive-thru. Menu focuses on hot and cold sandwiches, soups, salads, and high-protein options; GC has finished the remodel.

data.status: targeting an opening in the coming weeks
data.short_description: Dedicated article on BB's Mocksville, a pizza restaurant and live music venue from Buddy Black and John Forbes. The space is expected to hold around 300 guests indoors with regular live music and New York-style pizza as the centerpiece.

data.event_date: Late May to early June (grand opening)
data.status: aiming for a soft opening in the coming weeks and a grand opening toward the end of May or early June
data.short_description: Dedicated article on The Energy Station nutrition shop owned by Samuel Bustamante, the third location in the group. Offers healthy shakes, energy refreshers, fruit bowls, waffles, and donuts with hours planned Mon–Fri 7 a.m.–7 p.m. and Saturday 8 a.m. onward.

data.store_name: SKOL Brewing Company (Valhalla Gaming Lounge expansion)
data.short_description: Dedicated article detailing the 30,000 sq ft Valhalla Gaming Lounge addition to SKOL Brewing Company, led by Gray Lane and Drew Tull. The expansion adds 40+ gaming options, a commercial kitchen, live music areas, and large-format TVs.

data.location: 811 S Main Street, Suite 206, Unit 811-106, Rolesville, NC 27571
data.status: targeting a late spring to early summer opening
data.short_description: Beer garden-style pub from Dan Colby and Liza David with approximately 2,000 sq ft of indoor space, a large outdoor area, and roughly 20 seats at the bar. Will not serve food directly but partner with nearby restaurants; features local craft beers, wine, and frozen drinks.

data.location: 20601 Torrence Chapel Rd Suite 106, Cornelius, NC 28031
data.short_description: Your Mom's Donuts is expanding to a third location in Cornelius under owners Dustin and Colleen Wanemacher, taking over the former Sweet Cakes Bakery space. The shop will offer donuts and a coffee program with limited seating; interior buildout is underway.

data.status: working through final inspections before opening
data.short_description: Dedicated article on The Jugo Bar's first street-level retail location. The 3,000 sq ft space will offer fresh juices, smoothies, smoothie bowls, algae offerings, and rooibos teas, plus two meeting rooms; planned hours Tue–Sat 8 a.m.–8 p.m.

data.location: 2286 W International Speedway Blvd, Daytona Beach, FL 32114
data.short_description: Korean fried chicken franchise location from owners Surya and Mary Rao, located across from the Daytona speedway. Fast-casual format with seating for 50–70 guests; menu includes wings, boneless chicken, kimchi, tteokbokki, and sides.

data.event_date: End of April to early May
data.status: aiming to open by the end of April; early May opening also possible
data.short_description: Casual full-service Chinese restaurant specializing in Peking duck and handmade soup dumplings, with seating for about 150 and a full bar. Chef was trained by Michelin-star-recognized Quanjude; menu also includes noodles and traditional Chinese dishes.

data.status: targeting a grand opening at the beginning of May
data.short_description: Pool hall concept from owner Scott Stoltz with five leagues expected to operate out of the venue. Space includes a full pro shop, darts, a golf game, a bar, and lounge area; food service will begin in limited format.

Bakery-cafe franchise from Rishy and Quint Studer opening on the lower level of their 24-unit apartment building alongside an adjacent marketplace. Counter-service format with about 45 indoor seats plus patio; menu includes breads, breakfast sandwiches, soups, salads, and baked goods.

data.status: targeting a early May to mid-May opening, pending final city inspections
data.short_description: Dominican restaurant from owners Rene Perez and Brenda Reyes (chef), with seating for about 40 in 1,500 sq ft. Menu features traditional Dominican dishes including stewed chicken, oxtail, mofongo, and chimi; beer and wine pending licensing.

data.status: targeting an opening by late summer, or at the very least by October
data.short_description: Cookie concept from owner Brisma Fermin opening in a walkable area near City Hall. No further menu or design details provided in this roundup article.
Fast-casual barbecue operation with a drive-thru from Robert Rukab and his partner Zee, taking over the former Mr. K's BBQ space. Opening targeted for May 1.
Second Potbelly Sandwich Works in the Jacksonville market from Patrick and Leah Pendergast. Their first Jacksonville location is set to debut in April at 9908 Old Baymeadows Road.
First Potbelly Sandwich Works in the Jacksonville market from Patrick and Leah Pendergast, debuting in April ahead of their planned second location on Beach Blvd.
Repeated mention from Florida roundup: bakery-cafe franchise from Rishy and Quint Studer opening on the lower level of their apartment building alongside a marketplace.
Repeated mention from Florida roundup: pool hall concept from Scott Stoltz. New venue targeting early May opening.

data.location: 3940 Buford Hwy NE Suite B101, Duluth, GA 30096
data.status: Targeting opening in a few weeks (pending permits)
data.short_description: Latin-Japanese fusion sushi restaurant from Wilson Giovanny Perez and Melina Castan, expanding to a standalone ~50-seat space with a sushi bar and drink bar. Menu includes ceviche, tacos, and Peruvian-influenced dishes.

data.location: 5380 Peachtree Industrial Blvd Unit 100, Norcross, GA 30071
data.short_description: Coffee shop centered on music and performance from Malvina McNeill, with events like poetry slams, karaoke, and open mic nights. Menu includes espresso drinks, pour-overs, cold brew, and pastries; planned Monday through Saturday.
Casual taqueria focused on authentic Mexican cuisine from owner Brayan Romero, offering dine-in, takeout, and delivery. Menu centers on street tacos and traditional Mexican dishes.

Family-run Peruvian restaurant from Mayra Chipoco and family with full-service, from-scratch cooking. Menu includes pan con chicharrón, chicha morada, and Peruvian desserts; planned seven days a week.

Full-service neighborhood sports bar from owner Tim McGivern with about 90 indoor seats and 35 patio seats. Menu features wings, burgers, wraps, and sandwiches; described as the state's first Wings Etc. location.

Restaurant-first bar and grill from owner Rob Burton with nearly 100 indoor seats and a 40-seat patio. Menu features scratch-made ribeye steaks, lobster rolls, crab cakes, Cajun pasta, and half-pound burgers.

data.location: 257 North Cattleman Drive, Unit 81, Sarasota, FL 34243
data.short_description: Scratch-kitchen concept with indoor-outdoor layout and nano wall design, seating about 145 guests. Menu features globally influenced dishes spanning Indian, Asian, Mexican, and Italian flavors.

Dedicated article: Crisp Kitchen & Cocktails relocated ~100 feet from Latta Arcade to Brevard Courtyard, beginning lunch service March 5. Dinner and cocktail service including a Japanese highball machine is expected in early April.

data.status: targeting a late May to early June opening
data.short_description: Dedicated article: Modern Indian restaurant from Anish Nair with 113 seats, a mezzanine for private dining, and a 14-seat bar. Menu centers on progressive small plates drawing from regional Indian flavors; cocktails and curated wines included.

Go Greek Yogurt is planning a new fast-casual location in St. Petersburg's Sundial redevelopment near the Pier. The menu will feature fresh and frozen Greek yogurt bowls, yogurt-based salads, and a self-serve frozen yogurt bar with customizable toppings. This will be the franchisee's first restaurant under the brand.

Gold Crest Distillery is a new craft distillery and tasting room concept from Mitch Patel focused exclusively on vodka. The approximately 8,000-square-foot space will offer guided distillery tours, production observation, and cocktail tastings with no restaurant component. The distillery is expected to operate around major downtown events near the Kia Center and Exploria Stadium.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: Cheez'd and Confuzed is transitioning from mobile service to a permanent stationary location, serving award-winning cheesy sandwiches from a converted 1964 Shasta Airflyte camper 5–6 days a week. The location will feature walk-up window ordering, picnic tables, and an on-site commissary kitchen, with lunch service from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m.

data.location: 2960 Ball Ground/Canton Hwy, Ball Ground, GA 30107
data.short_description: Empire Steak South is a new food truck concept from owner Daniel Ray focused on hanger steaks over fries with sauces like chimichurri. It will operate alongside Cheez'd and Confuzed at the Ball Ground stationary location. The article does not specify an exact opening date.

data.location: 2110 Powers Ferry Rd Suite 120, Atlanta, GA 30339
data.short_description: Big Fat Vegan is a vegan concept from mother-daughter team Tanetta Ellzey and Iyonna Poags, marking the brand's first permanent brick-and-mortar location after 2–3 years as a food truck and pop-up. The café will seat up to 54 guests with indoor and outdoor patio seating, serving lunch and dinner Monday through Saturday. Menu highlights include a vegan Philly, jerk tacos, the Big Fat Vegan burger, and house-made vegan meat alternatives.

data.location: 290 Nicholas Pkwy NW Unit 5, Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.short_description: Sazon To Go is a fast-casual Latin bowl concept focused on takeout and delivery with limited seating. The menu features customizable bowls with proteins like chicken, salmon, steak, and lamb, plus sides like plantains. The business is owned by Naylin Zamora.

Burrito Shak is a fast-casual coastal Mexican concept with a build-your-own service line and approximately 45 seats, offering dine-in, takeout, and a pickup window. The menu includes burritos, bowls, tacos, quesadillas, and nachos with locally sourced shrimp. It is family- and veteran-owned by Antoinette, Phil, and Vito Basso.

data.location: 122 Chiquita Blvd. S, Unit 3, Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.short_description: The Dominican Experience is a fast-casual Dominican restaurant with a service line setup and approximately 24 indoor seats, open for breakfast, lunch, and dinner. Menu highlights include mangú, pollo guisado, mofongo, and traditional rice and beans. It is owned by Arthur Sanchez with Chef Arlene Ovalles.

data.location: 1189 SW Pine Island Rd., Cape Coral, FL 33991
data.status: Targeting late May or early June opening
data.short_description: Coffee Rush is a dual-lane drive-thru coffee concept with walk-up windows and limited outdoor seating. The menu features custom coffee drinks, white chocolate mochas, caramel drinks, breakfast sandwiches, and other items. No further ownership details are provided in the article excerpt.

data.status: targeting an opening by late summer, or at the very least by October
data.short_description: Cookie Head is a quick-service cookie shop with a service window, outdoor seating, and an open kitchen concept, owned by classically trained chef Brisma Fermin. The rotating monthly menu includes cookies, milkshakes, coffee, cookie sandwiches, and catering options. This is part of an ongoing franchise expansion with existing South Florida locations in Fort Lauderdale, Plantation, and Boca Raton.

data.location: 10108 SW Discovery Way, Port St. Lucie, FL 34987
data.status: soft opening around April 1 and a grand opening around April 20
data.short_description: Swift Grill is opening its second location as a hybrid concept combining a fast-casual build-your-own side with a separate dine-in tapas menu and bar service, seating approximately 121 guests. Shawarma remains the menu foundation, supplemented by Romanian, Greek, and Middle Eastern tapas, a full liquor menu, and THC cocktails. Owner Vlad Bradescu said the Port St. Lucie location allows him to execute the original fast-casual vision that the Fort Pierce location could not accommodate.

Kush Wynwood is reopening after a two-year closure caused by extensive structural renovations to its nearly 100-year-old building, with owners Matthew and Priscilla Kuscher targeting a June or July reopening. The full-service restaurant will offer approximately 40 indoor seats plus outdoor seating, with a focus on burgers, sandwiches, salads, local beer, and scratch-made items. The concept is described as a casual, Miami-centric dining experience with a high-level hospitality approach.

data.location: 6901 Collins Ave, CU1, Miami Beach, FL 33141
data.status: An exact opening date has not been announced
data.short_description: Crema Gourmet Espresso Bar is expanding to Miami Beach with its 27th location, operated as a fast-casual European café with a full kitchen, approximately 60 indoor seats, and additional patio seating for a total capacity of roughly 100. The menu features over 20 breakfast options alongside lunch and dinner offerings, an espresso program using Illy coffee, and items such as acai bowls, salads, sandwiches, and pasta. Co-owners are Efthymios Paliouras and Ioannis Sotiropoulos.

Big Jake's BBQ is a fast-casual barbecue concept with a drive-thru and limited indoor seating for about 12 guests, opening in the former Mr. K's BBQ space. The menu centers on pit-cooked chicken, ribs, and classic sides like macaroni and cheese, beans, potato salad, and coleslaw. Owner Robert Rukab is partnering with Zee, who has 25 years of restaurant experience, marking their first dedicated barbecue venture together.

Jatski's Ice Cream is a scratch-made ice cream shop producing all ice cream on-site, with a 1,200-square-foot space seating up to 12 guests, owned by Amy and Bill Laakkonen. The menu includes house-made flavors, sundaes, root beer floats, waffle cones, ice cream cakes, milkshakes, and pup cups, with some Finnish specialty items. The shop name comes from a Finnish slang word for ice cream, reflecting the owners' heritage.

data.event_date: By end of April (week of April 20)
data.status: opening expected by the end of April
data.short_description: Hopkins' Eatery is opening its fourth Tallahassee location, a locally owned sandwich and salad shop that has operated in Tallahassee for more than 40 years. This location is notable for being the first Hopkins' Eatery with a grill, bar seating, and alcohol service, with between 75 and 99 seats including outdoor seating. Owner Jacob Ogden said the Bannerman Crossings area was selected due to its growth and activity.
